messages.content:
**今天 5:16 PM**
精简我的ACL投稿  
\indent \textbf{Evaluated Benchmarks.} We evaluate LaViT on a diverse set of benchmarks designed to stress-test visual perception and latent reasoning. We focus on fine-grained details using MMVP~\cite{tong2024eyes} and the Attribute Recognition subset of V$^*$~\cite{wu2024v}, which specifically target ``CLIP-blind'' patterns and subtle object properties often missed by standard encoders. For higher-order cognition, we select the Relative Depth, IQ-Test, Relative Reflectance, and Spatial Relation tasks from BLINK~\cite{fu2024blink}, requiring the model to perform mental manipulation and geometric reasoning. Additionally, we include MMStar~\cite{chen2024we} to verify that our model relies on genuine visual understanding rather than language priors.

\indent \textbf{Evaluated Benchmarks.} We evaluate LaViT on diverse benchmarks covering fine-grained perception and latent reasoning. For subtle visual details, we use MMVP~\cite{tong2024eyes} and the Attribute Recognition subset of V$^*$~\cite{wu2024v}, which target CLIP-blind patterns and object attributes. For higher-level cognition, we adopt BLINK~\cite{fu2024blink} tasks on Relative Depth, IQ-Test, Relative Reflectance, and Spatial Relation, which require mental manipulation and geometric reasoning. We also include MMStar~\cite{chen2024we} to test robustness against language priors and ensure genuine visual understanding.
